---
title: "Public Finance in the Age of AI: A Primer"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    toc-title: "Sections"
    page-layout: article
---

```{=html}
<div style="margin-bottom:1rem;">
  <a href="../index.html" style="font-size:0.85rem; color:#6c757d; text-decoration:none;">← Back to all papers</a>
</div>
<div style="display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:0.4rem; align-items:center; margin-bottom:1rem;">
  <span class="score-badge score-mid" style="font-size:0.9rem; padding:0.2rem 0.6rem;">6.8 / 10</span>
  <span class="source-tag">NBER</span>
  
</div>
```

**Authors:** Anton Korinek, Lee Lockwood

**Published:** 2026-02-25 · [View on NBER](https://www.nber.org/papers/w34873){target="_blank"}
· [PDF](https://www.nber.org/system/files/working_papers/w34873/w34873.pdf){target="_blank"}

---

## Abstract

Transformative artificial intelligence (TAI) - machines capable of performing virtually all economically valuable work - may gradually erode the two main tax bases that underpin modern tax systems: labor income and human consumption. We examine optimal taxation across two stages of artificial


---

::: {.callout-note}
## Analysis Pending
This paper has been relevance-scored (6.8/10).
Full analysis is generated for papers scoring ≥ 7.5.
:::
